pattern: A two-state dynamic program over positions: at each index decide swap-or-keep, carrying the best cost for both choices given the previous index's choices.
hints: At each index you only ever choose to swap that column or leave it; the order of earlier choices does not matter, only their costs.
Keep two running minimums: the cheapest way to make the prefix valid ending with this column swapped, and the cheapest ending with it kept.

Transitions depend on comparing the two columns to their predecessors: when the strict-increase already holds you may keep both or swap both; when only the crossed comparison holds you must swap exactly one of the two adjacent columns.

edge_inputs.description: one swap at the tail fixes both sequences
minimum length already strictly increasing, no swaps
equal-valued columns force a swap to break the tie
swapping every column is the only fix
a position where both keep and swap stay valid

stress.description: construct two strictly increasing arrays then randomly swap some columns so a valid solution always exists
stress.note: guarantees solvability since the arrays are a column-permutation of two increasing sequences
